messages.content: say it betterv :I also noticed that there is a heavy focus on internal administrative tasks with a very few part of recruitment.
I have also observed a disproportionate emphasis on internal administrative duties compared to the limited attention given to recruitment efforts.
make it better
It appears that internal administrative tasks receive a significant amount of attention, while recruitment efforts seem to be given much less priority.
I also noticed that there is a heavy focus on internal administrative tasks with a very few part of recruitment. the internal process at Huningue is very huge to digest
I have observed a pronounced emphasis on internal administrative tasks, leaving recruitment efforts largely neglected. The complex internal processes at Huningue can be overwhelming to comprehend.
I didn't realized that the mission was focus mostly on administrative task with a very few task of rec

I was unaware that the primary focus of the mission revolved around administrative tasks, with recruitment activities being given minimal attention. It has come to my attention that the significant workload of internal processes at Huningue can be quite overwhelming to digest.
I didn’t realized that the massive amount of internal admin such as internal move  with a very very very little task of rec
I hadn't fully grasped the extent of internal administrative tasks, particularly the extensive internal moves, which seem to overshadow the exceedingly limited focus on recruitment activities.
say it differently

I had not previously comprehended the sheer magnitude of internal administrative responsibilities, particularly the extensive internal moves. Regrettably, there appears to be a disproportionately minimal emphasis on recruitment tasks.
say differently showing that you want try to make the effort but that you will need support

While I am willing to make an effort to handle the substantial workload of internal administrative tasks, including internal moves, I must admit that I will require support in order to effectively manage the limited amount of recruitment responsibilities.

say it better including the fact that I am willing to help of course, just didn't realize that the mission would be mostly focus on local and internal administrative + on boarding task rather than Talent acquisition strategy...  As it's not my speciality I will need internal support for the first months to make sure that no mistake is done

Although I am certainly eager to contribute and assist, I had not anticipated that the mission would predominantly revolve around local and internal administrative tasks, as well as onboarding activities, rather than focusing on developing a robust Talent acquisition strategy. Given that it is not my area of expertise, I kindly request internal support during the initial months to ensure that no mistakes are made.
make it the same but in a form of casual conversation

You know, I'm totally up for lending a hand, but I didn't realize that this gig would mainly involve handling internal administrative stuff, like local moves and onboarding tasks, rather than focusing on talent acquisition strategy. Since it's not exactly my area of expertise, I'll definitely need some internal support in the first few months to avoid any mishaps. Just want to make sure we're all on the same page!
more pro

Certainly! I'm more than willing to offer my assistance. However, it caught me off guard that the mission primarily centers around managing internal administrative tasks, such as local moves and onboarding duties, instead of focusing on developing a comprehensive talent acquisition strategy. As this is not my area of specialization, I kindly request some internal support during the initial months to ensure that all tasks are successfully handled without any potential errors.
more pro

I am fully committed to supporting the mission, and I am open to lending my assistance. Nonetheless, it came as a surprise to me that the primary focus lies heavily on managing internal administrative tasks, such as local moves and onboarding responsibilities, rather than prioritizing the development of a robust talent acquisition strategy. Given that talent acquisition is not my area of expertise, I humbly request internal support during the initial months to ensure flawless execution of all tasks and to avoid any potential mistakes.
more casual

I'm totally ready to help out however I can, but I gotta admit I didn't realize that the mission would mostly revolve around handling internal admin stuff, like local moves and onboarding tasks, rather than focusing on talent acquisition strategy. Since it's not really my expertise, I'll definitely need some internal support in the beginning to make sure I don't mess anything up. Just wanna keep things smooth sailing, you know?

keep it the same including that the training on the process is very specific and heavy on information, so I won't be able to give a big support on recruitment if I don't have an assistance from the HR team

I'm totally on board to help out, but I gotta be honest—I didn't expect the mission to primarily involve this level of specific and information-heavy training on internal processes. Given the focus on that, I might not be able to provide substantial support on recruitment unless I have assistance from the HR team. It would be great if we could work together closely to ensure a smooth transition and avoid any potential shortcomings in the recruitment process.
make it the same including that being part of internal ad,inistrative move and process it's not my speciality, My expertise is TA

While I'm eager to contribute, I just want to mention that my expertise lies primarily in talent acquisition, rather than in handling internal administrative moves and processes. So, I may not be able to provide extensive support in those areas without the assistance of the HR team. I'd greatly appreciate their guidance to ensure a seamless integration and to uphold the quality of recruitment efforts while leveraging my expertise.
more casual

I'm totally up for helping out, but I have to admit that handling internal administrative moves and processes isn't exactly my forte. My specialty is in talent acquisition, so I might need some extra support from the HR team to really make a solid impact in that area. Just want to make sure we're all on the same page and can work together to bring the best results!
include that I didn't realize that you wanted me to do this

Hey, just to clarify, I didn't realize that you wanted me specifically to handle all these internal administrative moves and processes. My expertise is more geared towards talent acquisition, so I might need some additional guidance and support from the HR team to ensure everything goes smoothly. I'm more than willing to give it a shot, but just wanted to make sure we're all clear on my role here.
make it more with more emphasis for her

Hey, just a heads up, I didn't quite catch that you wanted me to take the lead on all these internal administrative moves and processes. My main strength lies in talent acquisition, so I might need a bit more support from the amazing HR team to navigate through these tasks smoothly. I'm totally willing to give it my best shot, but I wanted to make sure we were on the same page about my role. Working together, we can make magic happen!
more pro and friendly

I wanted to mention that I may have misunderstood your expectations regarding my involvement in the internal administrative moves and processes. My expertise primarily lies in talent acquisition, and I am more confident in providing support in that area. To ensure a successful outcome, I would greatly appreciate the support and guidance from the HR team as I navigate through these tasks. I am fully committed to doing my best and collaborating effectively to achieve our goals. Thank you for your understanding and collaboration!
make it as it's your direct superior; more respectfull

I wanted to respectfully communicate that I may have misconstrued your intentions regarding my role in handling the internal administrative moves and processes. I want to acknowledge that my expertise mainly lies in talent acquisition, and I feel more confident contributing in that area. To ensure the best possible outcomes, I kindly request your support and guidance as I navigate through these tasks. I am fully committed to giving my utmost efforts and collaborating closely with you to achieve our shared objectives. Thank you for your understanding and mentorship in this matter.
more friendly

I just wanted to chat with you and let you know that I may have misunderstood how you wanted me to handle the internal administrative moves and processes. I want to acknowledge that my expertise lies more in talent acquisition, and that's where I feel most comfortable contributing. If it's cool with you, I'd really appreciate your support and guidance as I navigate these tasks. I'm totally committed to giving it my all and working closely with you to achieve our goals. Thanks for being awesome and understanding!

keep this form  including that it's not my area of expertise: I’m totally on board to help out, but I gotta be honest—I didn’t expect the mission to primarily involve this level of specific and information-heavy training on internal processes. Given the focus on that, I might not be able to provide substantial support on recruitment unless I have assistance from the HR team. It would be great if we could work together closely to ensure a smooth transition and avoid any potential shortcomings in the recruitment process.

I wanted to have a quick discussion and be transparent with you. While I'm definitely enthusiastic about helping out, it turns out that the mission's main focus entails an extensive amount of specific, information-heavy training on internal processes. As this isn't exactly my area of expertise, I might not be able to offer substantial support in recruitment without some assistance from the skilled HR team. I genuinely believe that by collaborating closely, we can ensure a seamless transition and prevent any potential challenges in the recruitment process. Your guidance and teamwork will be truly appreciated!
kepp it the same more casual

Just wanted to have a quick chat and keep it real with you. I'm totally on board to help out, but I gotta be honest—I didn't expect the mission to mainly revolve around this intense and detailed training on internal processes. Since that's not exactly my jam, I might not be able to provide major support in recruitment without some backup from the HR team. But hey, if we work together closely, we can make sure the transition goes smoothly and avoid any hiccups in the recruitment process. Your guidance and teamwork would be super valuable to me!
